item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but the crosslink to 2906 feels forced and doesn't make a lot of sense. Still upvoting, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the totally offbeat nonsense of it. I love the character bits. I love the grounding in reality — for some reason, the Foundation interacting with real life figures, as carefully infrequently as it happens, tends to be solid gold. This is good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…all enthusiastically making this exquisitely useless object somebody else's problem now."
I wanna read that, could be a supplement even

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does the MC&D crosslink link to your comment in the discussion forum instead of to the page proper?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that bit was great. It harkens back to a simpler time when the POTUS merely used the wrong sword on occasion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fun scip. The George W. Bush part was great… except for the cheap "recontaminated" gag. Broke my suspension of disbelief right away, especially seeing as it came right at the start of the log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What really made this article was George W Bush just having Enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, the part with George Bush was gold. Yeah, it's a fun read and I give you my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how the US president has to deal with wars, politics and moon snakes, and now a shitty magic golf ball follows him around to ruin his day. The small scale pettiness of this makes it for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels a bit unfinished in tying it all together, but I do so enjoy conversation section and what it does to it's unfortunate targets.

It gets a tentative +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked this as a draft, like it as a skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, I kinda like it. Seems like some fun worldbuilding. Could use some improvement, especially with regards to the MCD thing and 'why' it does it. But there's an interesting core.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Written for LordStonefish's 2017 Challenge.

I deeply appreciate getting feedback from Hydrozen, ReCursive, stormbreath, DrChandra, taylor_itkin, and a wee bit from MaliceAforethought.

How did this fucking thing even get here
Close this damn collapsible right f-

As soon as I saw the title "It's just fucking golf" in the challenge thread I had the germ of an idea: a piece of anomalous garbage created by some nouveau riche asshole in a moment of pique. So I googled "oldest country club in the US" and started to read about Foxburg Country Club. The essay on Foxburg's 2007 Historic Properties Nomination Form mentioned HJ Crawford by name. A young hotshot oil baron who'd recently become one of the wealthiest individuals on the planet is exactly the sort of person to get in touch with MC&D about a petty golf argument he'd had with a friend - and then try to haggle with them.

Trying to figure out how this thing ended up contained by the Foundation, rather than the UIU, had me spinning my gears for a moment. Then I realized Foxburg was made a historic property in 2007. In real life it remained under private ownership, but as a golf-related skip I could not pass up on the comedy potential of (briefly) nationalizing it. If it weren't for stormbreath I would likely never have known about the similarities between this and 2762. A tongue in cheek crosslink that gives a site director a chance to tweak the UIU's nose is better than accidentally replicating a Series III article, right?

I slashed large amounts of supplementary documents late in the editing process, as the docs I added only expanded slightly on information contained in the skip itself. They described the Fox family, J. Edgar Hoover, and (in 2007) Robert Mueller all enthusiastically making this exquisitely useless object somebody else's problem now.

I have some ideas for how to better expand / connect these events to other stories, but am always open to suggestions!

Edit: Added GoI tags and slightly expanded the Fox/Crawford story at the very end of the article. Hope that ties it together better.
